mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent within a single quarter, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate revenue contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity and credit demand, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint index of INDPRO and HOUST declines fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid monetary tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of contracting real GDP on corporate profit margins, producing strongly negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp while GDPC1 contracts, contradicting the predicted negative direction.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand and wage-driven consumption, which supports corporate earnings revisions and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 across three consecutive rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a year-over-year growth rate of 3.0 percent, inflation expectations become unanchored, forcing equity risk premiums higher and depressing valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline when CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 3.0 percent (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and declining real savings signals a credit-driven demand shift that ultimately exhausts consumer purchasing power, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative SPX returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample windows, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trajectory in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy supply-side utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline.
